title: Silent-run detector: suppress repeat alerts when a closed review issue exists for same runId
description:
## Problem

Paperclip's silent-run detector created **three** review issues ([QUA-30](/QUA/issues/QUA-30), [QUA-31](/QUA/issues/QUA-31), [QUA-32](/QUA/issues/QUA-32)) for the **same** `originRunId=d0cf417b-5c49-4234-85f9-de771b1a42df` within ~8 minutes (05:31Z, 05:33Z, 05:36Z). All three were triaged by CEO and closed as benign false positives.

Current dedup fingerprint includes the runId, but evidently it only suppresses repeat alerts while a *prior alert is still open*. Once a prior review issue moves to `done`/`cancelled`, the next heartbeat tick re-fires for the same runId.

## Why this matters

- Each alert wastes a CEO heartbeat on the same triage decision.
- Drowns the review queue and trains us to ignore the alert class.
- The detector is correct that the codex_local process is idle — but the actual decision ("benign, no recovery") has already been recorded against the runId.

## Acceptance criteria

- After a review issue for `originRunId=X` reaches a terminal status (`done` or `cancelled`), the silent-run detector MUST NOT create a new review issue for that same `originRunId` in subsequent ticks.
- Optional: instead of a new issue, append a comment to the most recent ter

2. **Stuck-session detector** — agent in `error` >30 min OR `last_heartbeat_at` >2h while `wakeOnDemand=true` → open issue assigned **CEO** with terminate-rehire recommendation.
3. **Bottleneck detector** — ≥2 P0 `in_progress` AND <5 runs/4h targeting those issues → post sharper-wake comment + on-demand wake the assignee.
4. **Token-budget detector** — weekly run-count × 4 > 90% provider cap → throttle timer-heartbeats company-wide + open OWNER notification.
5. **Recursive-wake detector** — ≥10 byte-identical comments on same issue in 60 min from same author → POST `/api/agents/<id>/pause` + open follow-up.

## Implementation

title: QUA-400/B — Pipeline-Op: 36-symbol .DWX backtest-matrix dispatcher + fail-fast schema
description:
**Owner: Pipeline-Operator. Parent: [QUA-400](/QUA/issues/QUA-400) Rules 2 + 4.**

## Goal

Update `dedup_index.json` schema to support phase-matrix dispatch across 36 .DWX symbols × 1 EA = 36 runs per EA per phase, distributed across T1-T5 (15-cap → 7-8 batches).

## Schema

```json
{
  "<ea_id>_<version>_<phase>": {
    "matrix": [
      {"symbol": "EURUSD.DWX", "terminal": "T1", "verdict": "PASS|FAIL|null", "evidence": "..."}
    ],
    "phase_verdict": "PASS|FAIL_NO_SYMBOLS_PASSED|null",
    "next_strategy_unblocked": "SRC<NN>_S<n+1> | null"
  }
}
```

## Behavior

- Validate every dispatch: `Symbol=*.DWX` else REJECT (Rule 1 enforcement).
- Tally PASS count across 36 symbols per phase.
- Apply Quality-Tech threshold (default `>= 1` for P2).
- PASS → open next phase issue for the EA.
- FAIL → write `verdict: FAIL_PHASE_<X>`, unblock next sub-issue under SRC parent, abort remaining phases for this strategy.
- All 5 MT5 instances run in parallel (round-robin per QUA-307 load-balancing).

## Acceptance

- Code + schema committed.
- One worked-example matrix run on a small input (3 symbols × 1 EA) showing fail-fast unblock.
- Closeout comment includes commit hash.

title: P0 — CTO framework deprecated-API patch + registry sync v2 across 4 worktrees
description:
## P0 critical — framework deprecated-API patch + registry sync v2

CTO QUA-303 review surfaced this — 2 deprecated-API warnings from framework headers will cause every Davey EA's compile to fail the V5 hard rule "Compile clean (no warnings, no V5 build_check violations)". 4 of the 5 Davey EAs are blocked behind this patch. Plus a second registry-sync gap found this heartbeat.

## Findings

### Framework-warning blocker (highest priority — gates ALL 5 P1 reviews)

CTO rejected [QUA-303](/QUA/issues/QUA-303) with:

```
QM_RiskSizer.mqh(163,46): warning 89: ACCOUNT_FREEMARGIN deprecated
QM_ChartUI.mqh(146,32):   warning 89: POSITION_COMMISSION deprecated
```

These are framework-side deprecations, not EA-side. Every EA that includes `QM_Common.mqh` (which transitively includes RiskSizer + ChartUI) will produce these warnings. Strict reading of the hard rule rejects all 5 Davey EAs.

Modern MQL5 equivalents (verify with current MetaTrader 5 docs):
- `ACCOUNT_FREEMARGIN` → `ACCOUNT_MARGIN_FREE` (account margin info enum)
- `POSITION_COMMISSION` → look up modern position-info enum or use `HistoryDealGetDouble(ticket, DEAL_COMMISSION)` for trade-level commission

### Registry sync gap v2

title: Strategy research workflow + EA enhancement loop + Pipeline-Op load balancing — OWNER directive 2026-04-27
description:
**Strategy research workflow — OWNER directive 2026-04-27 (parent issue, decomposes into 6 children)**

OWNER directive 2026-04-27 ~14:30 local. Codify how V5 actually executes the research-to-live arc, end to end. CEO is the parent owner; child issues are dispatched per agent.

## Why this exists

OWNER specified a structured, sequential, low-parallel research workflow to (a) save tokens, (b) keep the company focused on one thing at a time, (c) reuse V4's prior knowledge of strategy taxonomy. The pattern is opinionated and supersedes any improvised flow CEO + Research might have spun up.

## The workflow (binding)

### Issue tree shape

- **Per resource (book / paper / blog / video) = ONE parent Issue.** Title pattern: `SRC<NN> — <Source citation>`.
- **Per strategy found in that resource = ONE sub-Issue under that parent.** Title pattern: `SRC<NN>_S<n> — <strategy-slug>`.
- **All sub-issues created as `blocked` EXCEPT the first one.** First is `todo` and gets actively worked.

[gate-test] R-051-1 (4): real incident with evidence. OWNER 2026-05-01 09:11 UTC comment on [QUA-665](/QUA/issues/QUA-665) named a `Command Check Error` line emitted during a long T1 smoke that nobody caught during the run. Pipeline-Operator currently tails the journal *after* tester completion, so in-run error signals are missed. This is the failure mode this issue fixes.

## Problem

Pipeline-Operator runs MT5 tester on T1-T5 by spawning `terminal64.exe` and waiting for the test to complete, then ingests the journal. During the tester run, the journal is not tailed live. If the tester emits a soft error (compile warning that becomes runtime, broken-symbol message, init-failure stack, broker-side `Command Check Error`, etc.), nobody surfaces it until post-run aggregation. By that point the run has already burned T1 time.

title: Token-Controller routine: Fix persistent run contention preventing issue completion
description:
**Problem:** Token-Controller heartbeat routine creates execution issues but multiple concurrent runs are generated for the same issue, causing run contention that prevents completing the execution issue.

Observations across 5+ heartbeats (2026-05-08T11:13Z through 2026-05-09T01:01Z):
- QUA-829, QUA-852, QUA-905, QUA-1000 all experience the same contention pattern
- Each issue is locked by a different run ID within seconds of creation
- concurrencyPolicy='skip_if_active' does NOT prevent the concurrent runs
- Token surveillance work completes successfully but issue updates fail with "Issue run ownership conflict"

**Root Cause:** The routine's concurrencyPolicy is not being enforced at the execution-issue creation level. New runs are spawned even when the previous execution issue is still active.

**Proposed Solutions (Priority Order):**

1. **Set routine catchUpPolicy='skip_missed'** (if not already set)
   - Prevents backlog of missed fires from queuing simultaneously
   - Simplest configuration change

2. **Increase heartbeat interval from 3600s to 7200s (2 hours)**
   - Reduces collision surface
   - Trade-off: less frequent token monitoring

3. **Separate routine from executi

title: CTO: diagnose adapter path-mangle bug (C:\QMepo → C:\QMepo) that errored Quality-Business #1
description:
## Failure

- Agent: Quality-Business `f2c79849` (`claude_local` adapter), now `status=error`.
- Run: `9aeedee1-5451-4429-a529-03875f196979`.
- Error: `adapter_failed - Could not create working directory "C:\QMepo": ENOENT: no such file or directory, mkdir 'C:\QMepo'`.
- Triggered while QB1 tried to start QUA-432 (QB onboarding 2).

## Hypothesis

Looks like a path-interpolation bug where the adapter took `C:\QMepo` (or similar) through a string interpreter that read `` as carriage-return and stripped it, leaving `C:\QMepo`. The substring `epo` is suspicious — exactly what remains of `epo` after `` is consumed.

## Asks

1. Confirm the source path the adapter computed for QB1 and which interpolation step lost the ``.
2. Patch the adapter so workspace paths cannot be parsed as escape sequences (e.g. forward-slashes throughout, or raw-string handling).
3. Smoke-test by re-spawning a QB-class agent under the fix and verifying it can mkdir its workspace.
4. Once confirmed environmental, advise on retiring QB1 (`f2c79849`) vs leaving it inert. QB2 (`0ab3d743`) is currently picking up onboarding work.

## Scope

Class-3 adapter/runtime defect. Not blocking T6. Affects future agent h
